summary:
One blocking issue: `phase-0-setup.md` Step 0.2a contains an append sub-case for resumes where the `## Visibility` section is absent from the scope file — but the Resume Check at the top of Phase 0 skips to Phase 1 entirely when the scope file exists, making that sub-case unreachable. The next developer debugging a missing `{{VISIBILITY_FROM_SCOPE_FILE}}` substitution will read Phase 0, find the append logic, and conclude it handles the recovery — but it never fires. The fix is to either remove the append sub-case from 0.2a or move it to Phase 1's Resume Check, which is where the resume path actually lands and where the Visibility section would be consumed.

Four advisory issues: (1) The `--auto` mode rule in the Label Pre-Gate is buried after the interactive decision tree — a developer adding a new label case to the interactive paragraphs will miss that `--auto` mode applies a different rule for the same case; move it to the top as a named block. (2) The `{{ISSUE_BODY_IF_PRESENT}}` placeholder in the adversarial lead template has no documented fallback for no-issue directional topics (post-conversation classification can fire without an issue); different agents will handle this differently without guidance. (3) The instruction to mention the adversarial lead in the checkpoint summary is in Section 1.2 (Persist Scope), after the checkpoint in Section 1.1 — the constraint should live at the point of use in 1.1. (4) The `1.1a` section numbering signals an insertion and will complicate renumbering if more sections are added.

The adversarial lead agent prompt itself is clear and complete: confidence vocabulary, calibration section, delimiter framing, and visibility inheritance all match the acceptance criteria. The classification signal list in 1.1a is unambiguous.
